recs.s: URGENT (5-day pattern, all 3 codex runs fail today): codex CLI 0.133.0 ships a default model id `gpt-5-codex-alpha-2025-11-07` that the api-proxy doesn't serve. aw_info.json says model `gpt-5.3-codex` but the CLI overrides it. Fix options: (1) pin codex-cli to a previous known-good minor that uses `gpt-5.3-codex` literally, (2) pass `--model gpt-5.3-codex` explicitly in the codex_harness invocation, or (3) whitelist the alpha id at the proxy. Until fixed, every codex workflow is a guaranteed wasted run.

NEW (first time Antigravity engine ran in audit): The conclusion step requires `parse_antigravity_log.cjs` at `${RUNNER_TEMP}/gh-aw/actions/` but the file isn't included in the harness payload. Bundle parse_antigravity_log.cjs alongside the existing parse_claude_log.cjs / parse_codex_log.cjs siblings in the actions folder shipped to the runner.

CARRY (day 2): Yesterday's recommendation has NOT landed — Avenger run today again hit max_turns=25 with the same Go 1.26.3 vs 1.24.13 + golangci-lint missing root cause ($1.05 wasted). Pre-install Go 1.26.3 and golangci-lint in the Avenger pre-agent step, OR bind-mount /opt/hostedtoolcache/go/1.26.3/x64/bin into the sandbox PATH by default. Confirm landed within 24h.
DE-ESCALATE: Zero squid failures across 174 runs today (was 2 yesterday). Move from `watch` to `observe.` Re-investigate only if it reappears.

CARRY+EXPAND: Daily Safe Output Tool Optimizer $6.05 today (up from $5.55, peak was $8.54) — flatlining rather than dropping. Add Documentation Unbloat to the same review queue ($3.55 today, 24m, recurring). Combined these two + Daily Code Metrics = 51% of daily spend ($13.36 / $26.23).

NEW: 1 long Deployment Incident Monitor run failed today (run 26411586422, 19.1m, copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6). The other 27 runs were 1-2s skip-fast triggers. Drill into the failure mode tomorrow; if a second long-run fails, escalate.
CARRY (day 5): Drift now 7-65 turns across 7 runs. No fatal failures. Sample a few low-turn and high-turn runs side-by-side to confirm it's PR-content-driven (legitimate) vs prompt-driven (fixable).
CONFIRM: Copilot CLI 1.0.52 stable across 56 runs today (day 3 zero ENOENT, day 3 zero anthropic-beta). Pin 1.0.52 as the minimum allowed version in CI to prevent regression to 1.0.48.

Block rate jumped 4%→17.9% but ~60% of blocks come from Smoke workflows that intentionally exercise blocked domains. Non-smoke spikes worth a look: Linter Miner 43 blocks, Daily Testify 31, Copilot Opt 36. Keep as 'watch'.
